title: dubautia raillardioides
intro: Dubautia raillardioides, also known as Ma'o hau hele, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as skin irritations and wounds. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, which can help to prevent infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and topical applications, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized effectively.
